Build /beheer (admin): a moderation queue page listing pending_edits + pending photos with current-vs-proposed diffs and photo thumbnails; Approve / Approve-with-edits / Reject Server Actions that apply the diff to the business row, promote photos from pending/ to live/, stamp updatedAt, write audit_log, and call revalidateTag('businesses') + revalidatePath(`/ondernemers/${id}`).

Build /beheer/ondernemers (admin): full business table with Invite-owner (creates owner_invite + sends magic link), Add, Close (status:'closed'), Feature/sortOrder controls — each writing audit_log and revalidating. Build /beheer/logboek rendering the audit_log feed, filterable by business.

Build /beheer/mijn-zaak (owner): pre-filled edit form for the allowed field subset (descriptions, DayHours editor, phone/website/socials/price/tags/specialties/perfectFor) + photo upload component. Saving writes a pending_edit (diff only) and uploads photos to the quarantined pending/ prefix via a validated Server Action (sharp: type/size check, resize, WebP, EXIF strip). Show 'awaiting approval' banner.
Wire notifications via Resend: owner-submitted → email admin with deep link; approve/reject → email owner (reason on reject); reuse the same provider for magic links. No SMS/push.

Update /aanmelden copy to position it as 'request an invite' (it stays a mailto lead form). Add a short GDPR/privacy note covering owner data + photo consent, and confirm Neon/Blob/Resend regions are EU. Verify a full SSG build still prerenders all pages and JSON-LD, and that an approval revalidates the live page within seconds without a redeploy.
risks: Migrating source-of-truth from businesses.ts to a DB is the biggest change; mitigate with a deterministic seed script and by keeping the TS Business type as the canonical schema so all downstream components and JSON-LD are unaffected. Keep businesses.ts checked in as a documented backup/fallback.

SSG/SEO regression risk if reads become dynamic. Must use `use cache` + cacheTag and ONLY invalidate on approval (revalidateTag/revalidatePath) — never make /ondernemers/[id] dynamic, or Core Web Vitals and prerendered JSON-LD degrade.

Server Actions are reachable via direct POST independent of the UI (per Next 16 docs) — every mutating action MUST re-check session + that the owner owns the targeted businessId, or an authenticated owner could edit another listing.
Auth.js v5 + Next.js 16 compatibility and the 'breaking changes' warning in AGENTS.md — pin versions, read node_modules/next/dist/docs before wiring auth, and test the magic-link flow end-to-end.
Magic-link email deliverability (links landing in spam) can block owner login; use a verified sending domain (DKIM/SPF) on Resend and the real ondernemersvandekamp.nl domain.
Uploaded-photo abuse / malicious files / oversized images hurting CWV; enforce server-side type+size limits, re-encode with sharp, strip EXIF, cap dimensions, store in quarantine until approved.

GDPR: owner emails + uploaded photos are personal data. Keep all vendors EU-region, add a privacy note + explicit photo-consent on upload, use the audit_log as the processing record, and support deletion (close → de-publish, purge on request).
Free-tier limits / cold starts (Neon scale-to-zero adds latency to admin/login, not to cached public pages). Acceptable at this scale; revisit only if owner traffic grows.
Bus-factor for a solo/committee operator: keep the admin allowlist as an env var of 2–3 emails so login isn't tied to one person, and document the approve/revalidate flow.

decisions.question: How should owners log in?
decisions.answer: Passwordless magic-link (Auth.js Email provider) only. No passwords to store/reset — lighter to operate and a smaller GDPR/security surface. Same mechanism for admin, gated by an email allowlist.

decisions.question: Self-signup or admin-issued logins?
decisions.answer: Admin-issued only. An owner_invite row pre-binds email→businessId, so a login can only ever edit that one listing. The public /aanmelden form becomes 'request an invite' (stays a mailto lead form).

decisions.question: Do owner edits publish automatically?
decisions.answer: No. Every edit/photo lands in a pending queue; the admin's Approve is the only path data reaches the public site. Reject and approve-with-edits are also supported, all logged.

decisions.question: Where does the data live now vs. proposed?
decisions.answer: Now: hand-edited src/data/businesses.ts (static array, no DB/auth). Proposed: Neon EU Postgres as source of truth, with public pages reading a cached loader (use cache + cacheTag) so they stay SSG.

decisions.question: How do approved changes go live without losing SSG/SEO?
decisions.answer: On approval the action calls revalidateTag('businesses') + revalidatePath('/ondernemers/[id]'). Pages remain prerendered and cached (CWV + JSON-LD intact); only the changed page refreshes, in seconds, with no Vercel rebuild.

decisions.question: Where do uploaded photos go and how is consent handled?
decisions.answer: To a quarantined pending/ prefix on Vercel Blob (or Supabase Storage), validated + WebP-re-encoded + EXIF-stripped via sharp, never attached to live imageUrl until approval. The owner uploading is the consent; admin still gates publication — matching the project's 'owner photos only with permission' rule.

decisions.question: What notifications, and via what channel?
decisions.answer: Email only, one provider (Resend): magic-link login, owner→admin 'new edit pending', admin→owner approve/reject outcome. No SMS/push/Slack at this scale (~96 owners).

decisions.question: How is the audit log implemented?
decisions.answer: An append-only audit_log table (ts, actor, businessId, action, before/after JSON) written by every mutation, surfaced at /beheer/logboek filterable by business. Doubles as the GDPR processing record; nothing is hard-deleted.

decisions.question: Where does the admin UI live?
decisions.answer: In the same Next.js app under /beheer (NL), server-rendered, protected by middleware + per-action session checks. ~4 pages: moderation queue, business list, owner self-edit, audit log.

audit.notes: Audit findings (current system): no auth, no DB, no image upload today — onboarding is purely the mailto form in /Users/jezza/kamp/src/components/AanmeldenForm.tsx (page /Users/jezza/kamp/src/app/aanmelden/page.tsx). All data is a single typed array `businesses: Business[]` in /Users/jezza/kamp/src/data/businesses.ts (~96 records, ~40 fields, JSON-style quoted keys = programmatically rewritable; the Business type already has owner-workflow-oriented fields: permissionStatus, verificationStatus, imageStatus, updatedAt, lastChecked, imageCandidateUrl). It's imported in 11 places (page.tsx, sitemap.ts, llms.txt route, categorie/[slug], ondernemers/[id], kaart, loop-de-kamp, over-de-kamp, praktisch, cadeaukaart, plus components) — all would repoint to a cached loader. Pages are pure SSG via generateStaticParams; closed businesses are already excluded (page filters status!=='closed' and 404s), so the Close action propagates correctly. package.json has zero auth/DB/storage deps. Image rendering in /Users/jezza/kamp/src/components/BusinessImage.tsx already prefers a local '/'-prefixed owner photo (next/image-optimised) over external URLs over a generative placeholder — so promoting an approved upload to a local path slots straight into the existing precedence. Next.js 16 docs confirmed at /Users/jezza/kamp/node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/01-getting-started/07-mutating-data.md (Server Actions; explicit warning that actions are POST-reachable and must re-check auth) and /09-revalidating.md (use cache + cacheTag, revalidateTag/updateTag/revalidatePath, on-demand invalidation) — these are exactly the primitives that let moderated edits go live while keeping the site SSG. No vercel.json; deploy assumed Vercel. AGENTS.md warns Next 16 has breaking changes — consult node_modules/next/dist/docs before writing auth/cache code. Project memory notes heavy multi-agent workflows hit the spend limit twice, so prefer the simplest infra (single Postgres + one email provider) over a CMS.

audit.area: Security, Privacy & GDPR audit for the planned owner-login / photo-upload backend (ondernemersvandekamp.nl, Next.js 16 SSG on Vercel)
audit.recommendation: Build the backend on Supabase (already in use for image hosting at fyhxnuuloxsxkdpppqie.supabase.co) with Supabase Auth (admin-issued magic-link / invite-only, no public signup), Postgres with Row-Level Security so an owner can only read/write their own business row, a "pending changes" moderation table that never touches the live SSG dataset until the admin approves, and Supabase Storage buckets with server-side validation + image re-encoding. Keep the public site 100% SSG/ISR: owner edits land in a draft table, admin approval triggers an ISR revalidate (revalidateTag/revalidatePath) — the public bundle never imports user-writable data, so XSS/SEO/CWV are structurally protected. Treat the GDPR gaps as the most urgent item: real owner names (22 records), 58 phone numbers and 3 emails are ALREADY published from scraped public sources with no lawful basis, consent record, privacy policy or cookie statement — fix this before, or in parallel with, building auth, because it is a live exposure today, not a future risk.
packages: @supabase/supabase-js (browser + server clients)
@supabase/ssr (cookie-based auth in App Router / Route Handlers + middleware)
zod (validate ALL owner input server-side before it hits the DB)
sharp (re-encode/strip-EXIF/resize uploaded images server-side; already a Next dependency)
isomorphic-dompurify or sanitize-html (ONLY if you ever store HTML; not needed if descriptions stay plain text)
@upstash/ratelimit + @upstash/redis (rate-limit login + upload + write endpoints on Vercel serverless)
Optional: resend or postmark (transactional invite/approval emails with a signed DPA)

decisions.question: Does any backend, auth, DB, server action or upload exist today?
decisions.answer: No. The site is 100% static SSG. The only route handler is /llms.txt. There is no middleware.ts, no 'use server', no cookies(), no process.env usage in app code, no DB client, no upload handler. All ~96 businesses live in the typed file src/data/businesses.ts and are baked into the build. So this audit is about what the new backend MUST enforce, plus pre-existing privacy debt already shipped to the public site.

decisions.question: What platform should the backend use?
decisions.answer: Supabase. It is already the image host (fyhxnuuloxsxkdpppqie.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/site-images/...). Reusing it gives EU-region hosting (choose/confirm an EU region — Frankfurt eu-central-1), Auth + Postgres + Storage + Row-Level Security in one low-cost, low-maintenance, GDPR-appropriate stack that fits a solo/small-committee operator. Supabase offers a signed DPA. Avoid standing up a second platform.

decisions.question: Is there an XSS risk in owner-submitted text today, and where are the sinks?
decisions.answer: Today text is safe: descriptions render via React JSX ({b.longDescription} in BusinessDetailClient.tsx) which auto-escapes. The real sinks to guard once owners can write: (1) dangerouslySetInnerHTML in JsonLd.tsx — owner text flows into JSON.stringify'd JSON-LD; a name/description containing can break out, so escape '<' as < before injecting (Next does not do this for you). (2) BusinessImage.tsx renders external imageUrl via raw bypassing next/image — once an owner can set imageUrl, a javascript:/data: URL or off-domain host becomes an abuse/exfiltration vector. Validate URL scheme = https only and host against an allowlist (your Supabase Storage domain). (3) websiteUrl/instagramUrl/facebookUrl rendered as links — enforce https scheme and add rel=noopener noreferrer.

decisions.question: Is personal data of owners being processed, and is there a lawful basis / consent?
decisions.answer: Yes, and there is NO documented lawful basis or consent. businesses.ts already contains 22 real personal names (publicPersonName, e.g. 'Jimmy Karaaslan', with publicPersonRole like 'eigenaar, publiek genoemd in blog'), 58 phone numbers, 3 emails — scraped from public sources (sourceUrls). publicPersonName is emitted into JSON-LD as founder:{Person}. 'Publicly available' is NOT a GDPR lawful basis. The /aanmelden form has a consent checkbox but only fires a mailto (no record is stored), and the footer Privacy + Cookiestatements links are dead (href='#'). This is a present, live GDPR exposure independent of the backend.

decisions.question: Will the new backend regress SEO / Core Web Vitals / structured data?
decisions.answer: Not if owner-writable data is kept out of the public build path. Pattern: owners write to a Postgres 'business' row + a 'pending_changes'/draft table; the public SSG pages read ONLY admin-approved data via a build/ISR data source; admin approval calls revalidateTag/revalidatePath to regenerate just the affected static pages. The public HTML stays pre-rendered, JSON-LD stays builder-generated from sanitized fields, and no client-side auth/JS is added to public pages.

steps: GDPR FIRST (live exposure, do now): Publish a real Privacy/Cookie policy (NL, AVG-compliant) and wire the dead footer links in src/components/Footer.tsx (currently href='#'). Cover: controller identity, data categories, purposes, lawful basis, retention, recipients/processors (Vercel, Supabase, any email/analytics), data-subject rights, complaint route to Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens.

GDPR personal data remediation: Treat publicPersonName/publicPersonRole/owner portraits/scraped phone+email as personal data. Either (a) obtain & record explicit consent per owner before publishing names/portraits, or (b) remove names/portraits until consent exists and only show the business NAP. Add a per-record consent flag (e.g. consentNameAt, consentPhotoAt, consentBasis) so nothing personal publishes without a stored basis. Keep imageCandidateUrl/scraped portraits unpublished (the data model already separates these — enforce it).

Auth: Supabase Auth, invite-only. Admin issues logins (admin invites / magic links); DISABLE public sign-up. One owner account is linked to exactly one business via a join (owner_id on business, or owner_business membership table). No password-based self-registration. Enforce email verification and short-lived magic links.

Authorization (own-record-only): Enable Row-Level Security on every table. Policy: owners can SELECT/UPDATE only rows where business.owner_id = auth.uid(); INSERT into pending_changes only for their own business; admins (role claim / admins table) can read+moderate everything. Never trust the business id from the request body — derive ownership from the session. Storage RLS: an owner can only write to a path prefix keyed to their business id; public read only on the approved/published bucket.

Moderation workflow: Owner edits and uploads go to a DRAFT/pending state (pending_changes table + a 'pending' storage bucket or status column), never to live data. Admin UI lists pending text + photos, approve/reject. Only approval copies into published fields + published bucket and triggers ISR revalidate. This satisfies the brief's 'moderate/approve before it goes live' and protects SEO.

Input sanitisation / XSS: Validate every field server-side with zod (length caps, enum checks for category/priceLevel/streetSegment, HH:MM regex for hours, https-only + allowlisted host for imageUrl/websiteUrl/socials). Keep descriptions PLAIN TEXT (no HTML) so JSX auto-escaping is sufficient. In JsonLd.tsx, escape '<','>','&' (e.g. replace < with <) before dangerouslySetInnerHTML to prevent JSON-LD script breakout. Add rel='noopener noreferrer' + scheme check on all owner-supplied outbound links.

Image upload abuse/limits: Accept only via authenticated server endpoint/server action — never a public unsigned upload. Enforce: max file size (e.g. 5MB), max count per business, MIME + magic-byte sniff (jpeg/png/webp only), reject SVG (SVG = XSS). Re-encode with sharp to strip EXIF (incl. GPS), normalize format, cap dimensions; store re-encoded output only. Serve from Supabase Storage public bucket; if you later use next/image, add that single host to remotePatterns (today raw is used precisely because remotePatterns is empty).

Rate limiting & spam: Add @upstash/ratelimit on login (per-IP + per-email, lockout/backoff), on upload, and on write endpoints. Since signup is invite-only there is no public registration to abuse, but still throttle magic-link requests. Add bot protection (Vercel/Cloudflare) on auth routes. For the existing /aanmelden mailto form, consider moving to a stored submission with a honeypot/Turnstile to log consent and stop mailbox spam.

Secrets/env: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY must be SERVER-ONLY (never NEXT_PUBLIC_, never sent to client) — used only in Route Handlers/Server Actions for admin/moderation. Use NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL + anon key on the client (RLS does the protecting). Store all secrets in Vercel project env vars (Production/Preview separated), never in repo. .env* is already gitignored — keep it. Add security headers via next.config.ts headers(): CSP, Strict-Transport-Security, X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy (none exist today).

Backups & retention: Enable Supabase automated daily backups / PITR (paid tier) and document a restore test. Define retention: deactivated owner accounts and their drafts purged after a set period; provide an account-deletion path (GDPR right to erasure) that removes owner PII + their portraits from Storage. Log moderation actions (audit trail) but keep that minimal.

GDPR processor agreements (verwerkersovereenkomsten / DPAs): Sign/record DPAs with every processor: Supabase (EU region), Vercel, and any email provider (Resend/Postmark) and analytics. Maintain a short register of processing (verwerkingsregister). Ensure EU data residency; if any processor is US-based, document the transfer mechanism (SCCs / DPF).

Consent for photos/portraits & image rights: For every published owner/staff portrait, store explicit, specific consent (who, what image, when, scope = website, revocable) — a checkbox at upload time tied to the record. Confirm the owner holds the rights to any photo they upload (warranty checkbox) and that any depicted persons consented (portretrecht). Stop hot-linking/scraping third-party images for publication — current data hot-links facebook.com, google.com, instagram.com, plaece.nl etc. via raw ; migrate to owner-supplied, consent-backed images in your own Storage.
risks: LIVE TODAY: Real owner names (22), phones (58) and emails (3) are scraped from public sources and published (publicPersonName -> JSON-LD founder) with no lawful basis, no stored consent, and dead Privacy/Cookie links (Footer.tsx href='#'). This is an existing AVG/GDPR breach exposure, not a future one — an owner can demand removal and complain to the AP now.

Hot-linking third-party images: imageUrl/imageCandidateUrl point at facebook.com, google.com, instagram.com, plaece.nl, tijdvooramersfoort.nl etc., rendered via raw (next/image remotePatterns is empty). This is both a copyright/image-rights risk and a privacy risk (referer leakage is partly mitigated by referrerPolicy='no-referrer', but the host still sees the request).

Once owners can write imageUrl, a missing https-only + host-allowlist check turns the raw in BusinessImage.tsx into an XSS/exfiltration vector (javascript:/data: URLs, off-domain tracking). SVG uploads, if allowed, execute script.
JsonLd.tsx uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML over JSON.stringify(owner data) with no '<' escaping — owner text containing </script> can break out of the JSON-LD block (stored XSS) unless escaped.
Service-role key leakage: if SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is ever exposed to the client (e.g. NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix), it bypasses all RLS — total compromise. Must stay server-only.

Authorization-by-body: trusting a business id sent from the client instead of deriving ownership from the session lets an owner edit another owner's listing (IDOR). RLS keyed to auth.uid() is the mitigation and must be enforced at the DB, not just the UI.
No moderation gate would let owner text/photos publish directly into the SSG dataset, regressing SEO/CWV/structured-data integrity and removing the admin approval the brief requires.
No security headers/CSP today; without a CSP the admin/owner authenticated pages have weaker defense-in-depth against injected scripts and clickjacking.

Spam/abuse on auth + upload endpoints without rate limiting (credential stuffing on magic-link requests, storage flooding) — low signup risk because invite-only, but request-level throttling still needed.
No documented backups/retention/erasure path yet — risk of data loss and inability to honor GDPR erasure/portability requests.
audit.notes: Evidence base (all absolute paths): current state confirmed static — only route handler is /Users/jezza/kamp/src/app/llms.txt/route.ts; no middleware/auth/DB/server-actions; .env* correctly gitignored (/Users/jezza/kamp/.gitignore); next.config.ts is empty (no headers, no images.remotePatterns). XSS sinks: dangerouslySetInnerHTML in /Users/jezza/kamp/src/components/JsonLd.tsx (line 9, JSON-LD) and /Users/jezza/kamp/src/components/BusinessImage.tsx (lines 41,68 placeholder SVG) plus raw at lines 43,56 (external image, bypasses next/image). Text currently safe via JSX auto-escape in /Users/jezza/kamp/src/components/BusinessDetailClient.tsx (line 72 longDescription). Personal data + schema: /Users/jezza/kamp/src/lib/schema.ts line 168-169 emits publicPersonName as founder Person; counts from /Users/jezza/kamp/src/data/businesses.ts = 22 publicPersonName, 58 phone, 3 email. Existing Supabase host in data: businesses.ts line ~7143 (fyhxnuuloxsxkdpppqie.supabase.co). Dead policy links: /Users/jezza/kamp/src/components/Footer.tsx lines 94-95 (href='#'). Existing consent checkbox but mailto-only (no storage): /Users/jezza/kamp/src/components/AanmeldenForm.tsx lines 32,83-87. Site/NAP config: /Users/jezza/kamp/src/lib/site.ts. The Business type already separates published vs candidate images (imageUrl vs imageCandidateUrl) and carries permissionStatus/verificationStatus/inclusionStatus flags — extend this with explicit consent timestamps rather than inventing a new model. Note: README claims '67 verified active businesses' while the brief says ~96 — minor data-doc drift, not security-relevant.

options.name: Supabase (Auth + Postgres RLS + Storage) + Next.js Route Handlers/Server Actions on Vercel
options.pros: Already the image host, so no new vendor; EU region + signed DPA available; RLS gives DB-enforced own-record-only authz; invite-only auth, storage, and moderation tables in one place; low cost (free/low tier) and low maintenance for a solo operator; keeps public site fully SSG with ISR revalidate on approval.
options.cons: RLS policies must be written carefully and tested (easy to get subtly wrong); service-role key must be kept strictly server-side; some learning curve for @supabase/ssr cookie auth in App Router.

options.name: NextAuth/Auth.js + a separate Postgres (Neon/Vercel Postgres) + Vercel Blob for images
options.pros: Flexible, well-documented auth; Vercel Blob integrates natively; can keep everything in the Vercel ecosystem.
options.cons: More moving parts and DPAs (separate auth, DB, blob); no built-in RLS — you must enforce own-record-only in app code on every query (more IDOR surface); abandons the existing Supabase storage already holding the images; higher maintenance for a small committee.

options.name: Headless CMS with built-in roles/moderation (Sanity / Payload / Strapi) as the owner+admin backend
options.pros: Editorial moderation, roles, media handling and image transforms out of the box; admin gets a polished moderation UI for free; content can feed SSG via webhook->revalidate.
options.cons: Per-business 'owner can edit only own document' is awkward in most CMS role models (document-level ownership often needs custom plugins); another vendor + DPA; can be heavier/cost more; risk of SEO/data drift if the CMS becomes a second source of truth alongside businesses.ts.

options.name: Keep mailto/manual only — owners email changes, admin edits businesses.ts by hand
options.pros: Zero new attack surface, zero auth/upload risk, no new processors; cheapest possible; matches today's flow.
options.cons: Does not meet the stated goal (owners must self-serve edit + upload, admin must issue logins); does not fix the existing GDPR gaps (consent record, privacy policy) on its own; manual editing of a 96-record TS file does not scale and is error-prone.

audit.area: Hosting, Deploy & Cost plan for ondernemersvandekamp.nl — adding owner-editable listings + admin moderation while keeping the public site SSG/SEO-strong
audit.recommendation: Stay on Vercel (Hobby free now, upgrade to Pro €20/mo only if/when commercial use requires it) and add Supabase EU (Frankfurt, free tier) as the single backend for DB + Auth + Storage. The public site stays SSG/ISR exactly as today; owner/admin areas are dynamic routes. Flow: admin invites owner (Supabase Auth magic link / admin-issued password) → owner edits ONLY their row + uploads photos to a Storage bucket → edits land in a `pending` state (draft column or a `business_edits` table) → admin reviews in /admin and approves → an approved change writes to the live row AND calls revalidatePath/revalidateTag (via a Supabase webhook or a server action) so the static page regenerates on-demand. This keeps Core Web Vitals and JSON-LD intact (public pages are still prerendered HTML), keeps everything in the EU for GDPR, and costs effectively €0/mo at this scale (96 businesses, low traffic) — realistically €0–25/mo. Supabase is the lowest-friction pick because it bundles Postgres + Auth (RLS row-level security maps perfectly to "owner edits only their own row") + S3-compatible image storage + image transforms in one EU-hosted free service, avoiding gluing together 3 separate vendors.

decisions.question: Where does the database live (free/low tier, EU)?
decisions.answer: Supabase Postgres, EU region (Frankfurt eu-central-1). Free tier covers this easily: 500MB DB, up to 50k+ MAU auth. 96 rows + an edits/audit table is kilobytes. Row-Level Security policies enforce 'an owner can only UPDATE their own business row' at the DB layer — the cleanest possible authorization model for this exact use case. Alternatives: Neon (great serverless Postgres free tier, but auth+storage are separate products you'd add) or Vercel Postgres/Neon-on-Vercel (now powered by Neon) — fine but you'd still bolt on auth + blob separately.

decisions.question: Where do uploaded photos live?
decisions.answer: Supabase Storage (S3-compatible, EU, free tier 1GB + on-the-fly image transforms/resizing). Owner uploads go to a 'pending' bucket/prefix; on admin approval the public URL is written to the business row's imageUrl. This replaces today's external/owner-emailed image pattern. Keep the public site's existing raw + generative SVG placeholder for un-approved listings so nothing regresses. Alternative: Vercel Blob (1GB free on Hobby) — simple but US-default; for EU residency Supabase Storage is the safer GDPR story and keeps everything in one vendor.

decisions.question: Where does auth live?
decisions.answer: Supabase Auth (EU). Use admin-issued invites: the admin creates the owner account / sends a magic-link or invite from the /admin panel — no public self-signup, matching the requirement that the admin 'issues' logins. Roles: 'owner' (sees only their business) and 'admin' (full list + moderation), enforced via RLS + a role claim. Next.js side uses @supabase/ssr cookie-based sessions in server components / server actions / proxy (middleware). Next.js 16's own auth guide lists Supabase as a recommended library, and recommends a Data Access Layer for authz checks — adopt that pattern.

decisions.question: How does an approved edit reach the static public page without a full redeploy?
decisions.answer: On-demand ISR. Public dynamic pages (home, /categorie/[slug], /ondernemers/[id]) switch from compile-time `import { businesses }` to reading from Supabase with `export const revalidate = <long, e.g. 3600>` (or tags). When the admin approves a change, the approving server action (or a Supabase DB webhook hitting a secured route handler) calls revalidatePath('/ondernemers/'+id) and revalidateTag('businesses'). Visitors keep getting fast prerendered HTML; the changed page regenerates in the background. JSON-LD/sitemap/metadata all keep working because they're computed in the same server render. Avoid `next build`-on-every-edit (slow, and Hobby has build limits).

decisions.question: Env vars / secrets?
decisions.answer: Vercel Environment Variables, scoped per environment (Development / Preview / Production). NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL + NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY are public-safe (RLS protects data). Keep SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (admin/moderation writes, used ONLY in server code) and a REVALIDATE_SECRET (for the webhook route) as encrypted, non-public, Production-only vars. Never expose service-role key to the client. .env* is already gitignored. Use Vercel's Supabase native integration to auto-sync the keys.

decisions.question: Preview vs Production?
decisions.answer: Vercel gives every push/PR a Preview deployment automatically. Point Preview at a SEPARATE Supabase project (or at least a separate schema) so test edits never touch live data, and add noindex on preview (Vercel sets x-robots-tag on preview URLs by default; keep it). Production deploys from main → ondernemersvandekamp.nl. This is free and already how the repo is set up to work.

decisions.question: CI?
decisions.answer: Vercel's Git integration IS the CI/CD: push → install → `next build` → deploy, with checks on PRs. Add a lightweight GitHub Action (free for this repo) running `next lint` + `tsc --noEmit` on PRs as a quality gate before merge. No separate CI service needed. Optionally enable Vercel build cache (already default) to keep builds fast.

decisions.question: EU data residency / GDPR?
decisions.answer: Supabase project created in eu-central-1 (Frankfurt) → DB, Auth (owner emails/PII), and Storage (photos) all stay in the EU. Vercel: the app's static assets/edge are global, but with no PII in the static layer that's fine; for the dynamic owner/admin functions, set Vercel Functions region to fra1 (Frankfurt) so server-side processing of PII stays in the EU. Add a short privacy/processing note since you now store owner accounts + uploaded photos. Both Supabase and Vercel offer DPAs.

decisions.question: Realistic monthly cost at this scale?
decisions.answer: €0/month is achievable: Vercel Hobby (free) + Supabase Free both comfortably cover 96 businesses, low traffic, occasional edits, ~1GB images, and a handful of owner logins. The only forcing function to pay is Vercel's Hobby license being non-commercial — for a real client/business site you should budget Vercel Pro at €20/mo to be license-compliant and get team access, analytics, and higher limits. Supabase stays free unless DB exceeds 500MB or you want the paid tier's daily backups/no-pause (~€25/mo). Net realistic: €0 to start, ~€20–25/mo if you want commercial-compliant + Supabase paid backups. Plus domain renewal (~€10–15/yr).

options.name: Supabase (EU) for DB + Auth + Storage + Vercel hosting [RECOMMENDED]
options.pros: One EU-hosted vendor covers all three needs (Postgres, Auth, S3 image storage + transforms) on a generous free tier; RLS maps exactly to 'owner edits only their own row'; admin-issued invites supported; Next.js 16 auth guide officially lists Supabase; least integration glue; ~€0/mo at this scale.
options.cons: Free-tier project pauses after ~1 week of zero activity (a low-traffic admin tool may hit this — mitigate with a cron ping or the paid tier); service-role key must be handled carefully server-side; another dashboard to learn.

options.name: Neon (Postgres, EU) + Auth.js/Better Auth + Vercel Blob
options.pros: Neon's serverless Postgres free tier is excellent (true scale-to-zero, branching for previews); Vercel Blob integrates natively; Auth.js is the most popular Next-native auth. Good if you want best-in-class Postgres specifically.
options.cons: Three separate concerns to wire and secure (DB + auth library + storage); you build the owner/admin authorization yourself instead of getting RLS for free; Vercel Blob defaults to US (EU residency needs care); more moving parts = more maintenance for a solo operator.

options.name: Vercel Postgres/Blob (Vercel-native, now Neon-backed) + Auth.js
options.pros: Everything in the Vercel dashboard/billing; zero cross-vendor setup; native integration with envs and previews.
options.cons: Auth still DIY via Auth.js; historically US-default regions complicate EU residency; you lose Supabase's bundled Auth+RLS+Storage convenience; can get pricier than Supabase/Neon free tiers if usage grows.

options.name: Headless CMS (Sanity / Payload / Strapi) instead of custom auth+DB
options.pros: Out-of-the-box content editing UI + media library + roles, less custom UI to build for the admin.
options.cons: Overkill for one editable entity per owner with strict 'edit only your own listing' scoping — CMS role models don't naturally express per-owner row ownership; Sanity has dataset/US considerations; self-hosting Strapi/Payload adds a server to run and patch; higher ongoing maintenance than the recommended stack.

options.name: Stay fully static + keep mailto onboarding (no backend)
options.pros: €0, zero new maintenance, no GDPR PII to manage, cannot regress at all.
options.cons: Does not meet the stated goal — owners cannot log in or self-edit, admin cannot moderate changes online; all edits remain manual code commits by the developer. Only viable as a 'do nothing' baseline.

packages: @supabase/supabase-js (DB/Auth/Storage client)
@supabase/ssr (cookie-based sessions in App Router server components, server actions, proxy/middleware)
zod (validate owner-submitted fields server-side before they hit the DB)
steps: Create a Supabase project in eu-central-1 (Frankfurt). Add a separate project (or schema) for Preview.

Model the schema: `businesses` table mirroring the existing Business type (40-ish fields, with hours as jsonb), plus `business_edits` (or draft columns) holding pending changes with a status enum (pending/approved/rejected), `profiles` linking auth users to a business id + role (owner/admin), and an `audit_log`. Seed it once by importing the current src/data/businesses.ts (write a one-off migration script).

Set up Vercel <-> Supabase native integration so SUPABASE_URL / ANON_KEY sync into Vercel envs; add SERVICE_ROLE_KEY and REVALIDATE_SECRET as Production-only encrypted, non-public vars; pin Vercel Functions region to fra1.

Write RLS policies: owners can SELECT/UPDATE only the row where business_id = their profile's business_id (and only via the edits table, kept in pending until approved); admins (role=admin) can do everything. Public read of the *live* data can stay anon-readable or be served entirely through the SSG layer.

MVP backend: build /login (Supabase Auth, admin-issued invite/magic-link — no public signup), and a single /dashboard owner edit form (server actions + zod validation) covering text, hours, contact/socials/price/tags, plus photo upload to a Supabase Storage 'pending' prefix. Edits write to business_edits as pending.

MVP admin: build /admin (gated to role=admin) listing pending edits + uploaded photos with approve/reject. On approve, a server action (using service-role key) merges the edit into the live row, moves the photo to the public prefix, sets imageUrl, then calls revalidatePath('/ondernemers/'+id) + revalidateTag('businesses').

Migrate public pages off the compile-time import: have home, /categorie/[slug], and /ondernemers/[id] read live data from Supabase with generateStaticParams + a long `revalidate` (e.g. 3600) and tag the fetches with 'businesses'. Keep JSON-LD, sitemap, llms.txt, and metadata generation reading the same source so SEO output is unchanged. Verify Lighthouse/CWV is unchanged (still prerendered HTML).

Configure next.config images.remotePatterns to whitelist the Supabase Storage hostname so approved owner photos can use next/image optimization (today external are unoptimized — opt approved photos into next/image for better LCP).

Set up the GitHub Action PR gate (next lint + tsc --noEmit) and confirm Preview deploys hit the Preview Supabase project with noindex. Document the env vars and the approve→revalidate flow in the README.

Add GDPR housekeeping: short privacy note (owner accounts + photos stored in EU), confirm DPAs accepted on both vendors, add a cron/uptime ping to keep the free Supabase project from pausing if traffic is sparse.
risks: Vercel Hobby is non-commercial — for a client/business site you must move to Pro (€20/mo) to be license-compliant; budget for this rather than treating €0 as permanent.

Supabase free-tier projects PAUSE after ~7 days of inactivity; a low-traffic admin tool can trip this, causing a cold first request or apparent outage. Mitigate with a scheduled ping (Vercel Cron) or the paid tier.

SEO regression risk if public pages are accidentally made dynamic (per-request) instead of SSG+ISR. Must keep generateStaticParams + long revalidate; verify prerendered HTML and JSON-LD remain in the static output after the data-source swap.

Migrating the source of truth from src/data/businesses.ts (11k-line typed file, currently the single source) to a DB is the riskiest step — needs a careful one-time seed migration and a way to keep the typed Business shape in sync (generate types from the DB or share a zod schema).
Service-role key leakage would bypass all RLS — it must live only in server-side code/Production env, never NEXT_PUBLIC_ and never in client components.
On-demand revalidation must be secured (REVALIDATE_SECRET) if exposed as a webhook route, otherwise anyone could trigger regenerations.
User-uploaded images are an abuse/cost/safety surface (size limits, content type validation, EXIF stripping, and the moderation step before going live are all required).
EU residency only holds if BOTH the Supabase project region AND Vercel function region are EU; the default Vercel function region may be US — must be explicitly set to fra1.
audit.notes: Audit confirms the current app is 100% static: data is a compile-time TypeScript import (`import { businesses } from \"@/data/businesses\"` in /Users/jezza/kamp/src/app/ondernemers/[id]/page.tsx and /Users/jezza/kamp/src/app/categorie/[slug]/page.tsx), pages use generateStaticParams (no `export const dynamic/revalidate` anywhere), there is NO runtime fetch, NO process.env usage, and NO next.config images.remotePatterns. External business photos are rendered with raw in /Users/jezza/kamp/src/components/BusinessImage.tsx (referrerPolicy no-referrer), so they currently incur ZERO Vercel Image Optimization cost — only approved owner photos would opt into next/image. Stack: Next.js 16.2.9, React 19.2.4, Tailwind v4, no DB/auth/env today. Source of truth to migrate: /Users/jezza/kamp/src/data/businesses.ts (~11,059 lines, 96 businesses, ~40-field Business type with structured DayHours[]). Next.js 16's bundled auth guide (node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/02-guides/authentication.md) officially lists Supabase, Better Auth, Clerk, NextAuth/Auth.js, Auth0, Stack Auth and recommends a Data Access Layer + proxy-based session checks. ISR/on-demand revalidation guidance (node_modules/.../incremental-static-regeneration.md) recommends a high revalidate time + revalidatePath/revalidateTag for precise on-demand updates — exactly the post-approval pattern proposed. \n\nBUILD EFFORT ESTIMATE — MVP (login + owner edits text/hours/contact + photo upload + admin approve/reject + on-demand revalidate, single Supabase project): roughly 4–7 working days for one developer familiar with Next.js App Router + Supabase. FULL SYSTEM (separate preview project, full RLS hardening, audit log, EXIF strip + image transforms, invite emails, GDPR copy/DPA, GitHub Action gate, anti-pause cron, type generation from DB, polished admin dashboard): roughly 2–3 weeks total. Lowest-friction reliable setup = Vercel + Supabase EU on free tiers, upgrading to Vercel Pro (€20/mo) for license compliance once it is a live client site.

plan.summary: Build the owner self-service backend on Supabase (EU/Frankfurt), kept strictly OUT of the public render path. The public site stays exactly as it is today — SSG over the typed src/data/businesses.ts, which we KEEP as the seed + permanent fallback — and gains an "overrides" layer: approved owner edits live in Postgres and are merged on top of the static record at build/revalidate time. A separate, noindexed portal (/login, /beheer for owners, /admin for the committee) handles auth and moderation. DECISION: Supabase over Payload CMS v3. Two of three stack-level specialists independently chose Supabase, and it fits THIS repo better: (1) the Business type ALREADY encodes the approval gate we need (imageStatus / permissionStatus / verificationStatus enums, plus the "imageCandidateUrl is stored but NEVER rendered until owner-approved" invariant) — so a pending/approved override row is a direct continuation of an existing rule, not a new CMS abstraction; (2) Payload v3 would force the whole app into a stateful server runtime and retire businesses.ts as source-of-truth, rewriting the data path for all ~27 consumers through its Local API — a far bigger, riskier change for a solo/committee operator; (3) magic-link (passwordless) auth is the lowest-support flow for the real scenario "owner emails admin, admin issues a login" to ~96 non-technical shopkeepers, and Supabase ships it with EU region, Storage, and Row Level Security on a free/low tier. EVERY mutation is a Server Action that re-checks session + ownership in a DAL (never the proxy, never a layout); RLS is the database backstop. Approved edits fire on-demand revalidation so only the touched pages regenerate — zero redeploy, intact JSON-LD, no Core Web Vitals regression. Confirmed against the in-repo Next.js 16 docs: middleware is now proxy.ts (optimistic cookie checks only, no DB), and revalidateTag MUST use the two-arg form revalidateTag(tag, 'max') (single-arg is deprecated); updateTag is available for immediate invalidation.

recommendedStack.choice: Keep Next.js 16 (16.2.9) on Vercel, EU region (fra1) for the serverless functions
recommendedStack.why: Public pages stay SSG/ISR exactly as now; only the new portal/API routes need the server runtime. Pin the function region to Frankfurt so auth/edit/upload handlers run in the EU. No new framework, no second service to operate.

Adds the write store the project currently lacks (today ALL data is a static TS file). businesses.ts stays the SSG seed + fallback; Postgres holds owner accounts, ownership mapping, and pending/approved overrides. Free/low tier covers ~96 businesses. EU residency + a DPA satisfies GDPR.

recommendedStack.choice: Supabase Auth — magic link (passwordless) primary, admin-issued invites
recommendedStack.why: Matches the real flow: admin adds the owner's email (invite row) and the owner clicks a one-time link — no password to generate, transmit, forget, or phish for non-technical shopkeepers. The in-repo Next 16 auth guide explicitly recommends a library over hand-rolled JWT/cookies. @supabase/ssr handles session cookies in Server Components / Actions / Route Handlers.

recommendedStack.choice: Supabase Storage (private 'owner-uploads' bucket + public 'business-photos' bucket), served via next/image with images.remotePatterns
recommendedStack.why: Same provider as DB+Auth = one EU project, one DPA, RLS on objects. Uploads land PRIVATE+pending; on approval the admin promotes the object to the public bucket and writes the public_url. Strip EXIF/GPS on ingest; sniff magic bytes; cap size/count. Reuses the existing imageStatus/permissionStatus semantics one-to-one.

recommendedStack.choice: Custom thin admin built in the app at /admin (no third-party CMS)
recommendedStack.why: The moderation surface is small and bespoke (approve/reject a diff of override fields + a photo). A hand-built /admin reuses the existing Business TypeScript type and Tailwind/lucide UI, avoids per-seat CMS fees for ~96 logins, and keeps everything in one repo. Payload/Sanity/Strapi would each be heavier than the job requires here.

ownerFlow: 1. Request access: owner uses the existing public /aanmelden page (today a mailto). This stays as the front door — it just emails the admin a request to be set up.

2. Admin issues login: in /admin the admin enters the owner's email and links it to their business_id; this calls supabase.auth.admin.inviteUserByEmail server-side (service-role key, server-only) and inserts an owner_business row. The owner receives an invite email.

3. Owner logs in: owner goes to /login, enters their email, gets a one-time magic link (signInWithOtp); the /auth/callback Route Handler exchanges the code for a session cookie and redirects to /beheer.

4. Owner edits: /beheer is a Server Component that runs verifySession() + lists only the businesses linked via owner_business. The owner edits descriptions, opening hours (using the existing structured DayHours UI), phone/website/socials/price/tags, and uploads/replaces/removes photos (hero + gallery). Mobile-first — owners use phones.

5. Submit for review: the submit Server Action re-runs verifySession() + requireBusinessAccess(businessId), validates with Zod, and UPSERTS a business_overrides row with status='pending' (it NEVER writes the live/published value). Photo uploads go to the PRIVATE bucket and create a business_media row with status='pending'. Owner sees a 'in afwachting van goedkeuring' (pending review) banner. Live site is unchanged at this point.
6. Admin approves (see adminFlow). On approval the override/photo flips to status='approved'.

7. Edit goes live: the approval action calls revalidateTag(`business:<id>`, 'max') (plus category/sitemap tags) — the affected /ondernemers/[id], its /categorie/[slug], home, and the map regenerate via ISR on next visit, no redeploy. The owner's change is now public with intact JSON-LD.
adminFlow: Sign in to /admin via the same magic link; the DAL gates the whole segment on profiles.role==='admin' (checked in each page/action, not just a layout).
Owners screen: invite an owner by email and bind them to a business_id (inviteUserByEmail + owner_business insert); revoke/disable an owner; reassign a business to a different owner.

Moderation queue: list all business_overrides and business_media with status='pending'. Each item shows a field-by-field DIFF (current published value vs proposed) and, for photos, the pending image with a 'never-rendered-until-approved' preview from the private bucket via a signed URL.

Approve: flips the override/photo to status='approved', stamps reviewed_by/reviewed_at, promotes the photo object from the private to the public bucket and writes public_url, sets the matching imageStatus='approved' / permissionStatus='owner_approved' / verificationStatus='owner_approved', then fires on-demand revalidation for the affected pages.
Reject: flips to status='rejected' with an optional reason (shown to the owner on /beheer); nothing publishes.

Full-list management: browse/search all ~96 businesses, edit any field directly as admin (same override mechanism, auto-approved), toggle featured/sortOrder, and mark closed/seasonal — i.e. the admin can do everything an owner can, for any business.
Audit: every override and media row carries submitted_by/submitted_at/reviewed_by/reviewed_at for a simple change history.
plan.dataModel: "KEEP src/data/businesses.ts as the canonical seed and permanent fallback; its exported Business interface remains the single shared TypeScript contract for the whole app. Postgres holds ONLY accounts + ownership + the editable overlay, never a full duplicate of the catalogue. Tables (all in EU project, all with Row Level Security ON): (1) profiles { user_id pk -> auth.users, role: 'admin'|'owner', display_name, created_at }. (2) owner_business { user_id, business_id (= Business.id string), role, created_at } — the scoping table; an owner may map to one or more businesses. (3) business_overrides { id, business_id, field-set mirroring the EDITABLE subset of Business (longDescription, shortDescription, hours jsonb as DayHours[], hoursNote, phone, websiteUrl, instagramUrl, facebookUrl, socials jsonb, priceRange, priceLevel, tags text[], specialties text[], perfectFor text[], dietaryTags text[], highlights text[]), status: 'pending'|'approved'|'rejected', submitted_by, submitted_at, reviewed_by, reviewed_at, reject_reason } — one current row per business; pending edits do NOT overwrite the approved values. (4) business_media { id, business_id, kind: 'hero'|'gallery', storage_path, public_url, width, height, blurhash, sort_order, status: 'pending'|'approved'|'rejected'|'replaced', uploaded_by, created_at, reviewed_by, reviewed_at }. MERGE RULE at read time: effective business = seed record from businesses.ts, deep-merged with its APPROVED business_overrides row, with APPROVED business_media replacing imageUrl (hero) and gallery[]; when no approved override/media exists, the page renders the existing businesses.ts values untouched — so all 98 records keep working and the site degrades gracefully. APPROVAL GATE reuses the type's own enums: a new owner photo lands as imageStatus='owner_photo_needed'/permissionStatus='owner_photo_needed' (pending) and is rendered ONLY after approval sets imageStatus='approved'/permissionStatus='owner_approved'/verificationStatus='owner_approved' — the same never-render-until-approved invariant that imageCandidateUrl already enforces. RLS: owners can SELECT/UPSERT only rows where business_id is in their owner_business set and only into status='pending'; admins do everything; the public/anon role reads NOTHING from these tables (the public site reads the merged data server-side through the service path at build/revalidate, not via the anon client)."
plan.publishStrategy: "Keep /ondernemers/[id] and /categorie/[slug] static via generateStaticParams (unchanged) and KEEP businesses.ts driving the param set, so the public render path and SEO surface (JSON-LD, sitemap, llms.txt, OG images, canonical) are byte-for-byte today's output until an edit is approved. Introduce a single data-access module (src/lib/businessData.ts) that all ~27 consumers import instead of importing the businesses array directly; for the public path it returns seed-merged-with-approved-overrides. Wrap the per-business read in a 'use cache' function tagged cacheTag(`business:<id>`) and the list/category reads with their own tags (requires cacheComponents:true in next.config.ts). On approval, the admin Server Action calls revalidateTag(`business:<id>`, 'max') plus the affected category/list/sitemap tags — CONFIRMED against the in-repo Next 16 docs that the two-arg form is required (single-arg revalidateTag is deprecated); use updateTag for immediate invalidation if the admin wants the change visible instantly. Affected pages regenerate via stale-while-revalidate on next visit — no full redeploy, no dynamic rendering of public routes, no CWV regression. The portal (/login, /auth/callback, /beheer, /admin, /api/*) is dynamic and excluded: noindex via a robots route-segment + 'X-Robots-Tag: noindex' header, and excluded from sitemap.ts. proxy.ts does ONLY an optimistic cookie check to redirect unauthenticated users away from /beheer and /admin — never a DB call (per the in-repo proxy doc); the authoritative session+role+ownership checks live in the DAL and run inside every page, Server Action, and Route Handler. Owner-uploaded photos are served through next/image with images.remotePatterns whitelisting the Supabase public bucket host. A small Vercel Cron pings Supabase to stop the free-tier project pausing."

phases.scope: Introduce src/lib/businessData.ts and migrate all ~27 consumers (pages, components, lib) from `import { businesses }` to functions from this module. It initially just re-exports the static array. Verify the static build, JSON-LD, sitemap, and llms.txt are unchanged before touching any backend. This isolates every later change to one seam.

phases.phase: Phase 1 — MVP: auth + owner edit of TEXT/fields + admin moderation
phases.scope: Provision Supabase EU; create profiles, owner_business, business_overrides with RLS; seed nothing (businesses.ts is the seed). Add @supabase/ssr wrappers, DAL (verifySession/requireBusinessAccess/requireAdmin), proxy.ts (optimistic cookie check), /login + /auth/callback, /beheer (edit descriptions, hours, phone/web/socials/price/tags) submitting pending overrides, /admin (invite owners + moderate text overrides with diff + approve/reject). Wire approval -> revalidateTag('business:<id>','max'). Enable cacheComponents + per-business cacheTag. Noindex the portal; add Vercel Cron keep-alive. NO photo upload yet — owners still request photo changes by email. This already delivers the core ask: an owner edits their own listing's text/hours and the admin approves it live.

phases.phase: Phase 2 — Full: owner photo upload + moderation + gallery
phases.scope: Add business_media table + private/public Storage buckets with RLS; drag-drop mobile-first upload UI in /beheer (hero + reorderable gallery, replace/remove); server-side validation (magic-byte sniff, type allowlist jpeg/png/webp/avif, ~10MB cap, gallery cap, EXIF/GPS strip); pending photos render NEVER until approved (reuse imageStatus gate). Admin moderation gains photo approve/reject with signed-URL preview and promote-to-public-bucket on approve. Add images.remotePatterns for the public bucket; serve via next/image. Optional: blurhash, owner-facing reject reasons, simple change history.

Authorization test suite proving an owner cannot read/write another owner's overrides or media (RLS + Server Action checks); DPAs signed with Supabase and Vercel; privacy/processing note; backups verified; rate-limit the magic-link and upload endpoints; admin audit view. Optional later: migrate businesses.ts fully into Postgres if the committee wants to manage the catalogue entirely in-app.

plan.costNotes: "Target: EUR 0-25/month at this scale. Supabase free tier (1 project, 500MB DB, 1GB storage, 50k MAU) comfortably covers ~96 businesses and a handful of admins — but the free tier PAUSES after inactivity, so add a Vercel Cron keep-alive ping; if pausing is unacceptable for the committee, Supabase Pro is ~USD 25/mo and removes it. Vercel: the existing Hobby plan may suffice, but commercial use + always-on server functions typically means Vercel Pro ~USD 20/mo; pin the function region to fra1 (EU). Image cost is the main variable: owner uploads + next/image optimization — cap per-file size (~10MB) and gallery count (~8) to bound storage/egress; Supabase Storage egress is cheap at this volume. No per-seat fees anywhere (the reason a per-seat SaaS CMS was rejected for ~96 logins). One-time: developer build time per the phases above. Recurring ops are low: pinned versions, occasional Supabase/Next upgrades."
risks: Architectural shift from purely-static to static-public + stateful-portal: there is NO backend today. The DB/storage must be kept alive (free-tier keep-alive ping), backed up, and patched. This is the single biggest change to the project's low-maintenance character — adopt it deliberately.

Authorization is security-critical: every Server Action MUST independently call verifySession() + requireBusinessAccess()/requireAdmin(); a hidden button is NOT access control. One forgotten check is a full authz bypass. RLS in Postgres is the mandatory backstop; ship the cross-owner denial test (scripts/test-authz.ts) before go-live.

Next 16 gotchas confirmed in-repo: (a) middleware is now proxy.ts and must do optimistic cookie checks ONLY — no DB/data fetching, and the matcher must exclude _next/static, _next/image, api, and public assets or it can block them; (b) revalidateTag single-arg form is deprecated — use revalidateTag(tag,'max') or updateTag; (c) the auth-in-layout trap — layouts don't re-render on client nav, so auth checks must live in the DAL per page/action, not in a single layout.

SEO/CWV regression if a public route is accidentally made dynamic or gated behind proxy/session. Keep /ondernemers/[id] and /categorie/[slug] static with generateStaticParams; use on-demand revalidation only. Verify JSON-LD/sitemap/llms.txt are unchanged after the Phase 0 refactor before proceeding.

Image moderation correctness: a pending photo must NEVER render. Reuse the existing imageStatus/permissionStatus gate and serve pending uploads only from the PRIVATE bucket via signed URLs in /admin. Validate server-side (magic-byte sniff, type allowlist, size/count caps) and strip EXIF/GPS on ingest for privacy/GDPR.
GDPR is paperwork + region, not region alone: sign a DPA with BOTH Supabase and Vercel, keep all data EU-resident, document the lawful basis and a deletion path for owner accounts/photos.

Refactor blast radius: ~27 files import the businesses array directly. Phase 0 funnels them through one module first; doing auth/photos before that seam exists would multiply the change surface and the regression risk.
openQuestions: Hosting commitment: stay on Vercel (simplest given current setup) or self-host? This affects whether 'use cache' runtime persistence and ISR behave as described, and the cost line.
Acceptable to run Supabase free tier with a keep-alive cron, or does the committee want Pro (~USD25/mo) to guarantee no pausing and backups?
Can one business have MULTIPLE owner logins (e.g. owner + manager), or strictly one login per business? (owner_business already supports many-to-many; confirm the policy.)

Which fields should owners edit themselves vs. admin-only? Proposed owner-editable: descriptions, hours, phone, website, socials, price, tags, specialties, perfectFor, dietary tags, photos. Admin-only: name, category, address, lat/lng, featured, sortOrder, status. Confirm.
Photo policy: max gallery count, max file size, allowed types (proposed jpeg/png/webp/avif; HEIC rejected with re-export prompt) — and do we auto-strip EXIF/GPS (recommended yes).

Should an approved edit go live instantly (updateTag) or is stale-while-revalidate on next visit (revalidateTag '...','max') acceptable? Instant is slightly heavier; SWR is the default recommendation.
Language of the portal/emails — Dutch only (recommended for shopkeeper audience) or NL/EN?

Long-term: keep businesses.ts as the seed/fallback indefinitely, or migrate the full catalogue into Postgres later so the committee manages everything in-app? MVP keeps the file; Phase 3+ optional migration.
Who are the admin(s) and what email(s) seed the first admin profile? Needed to bootstrap /admin access.
Email deliverability: use Supabase's built-in magic-link email, or wire a custom SMTP/sender on the ondernemersvandekamp.nl domain for better inbox placement and branding?
